summary: Implement M3 tribute and troop obligation fulfillment
acceptance_criteria: M3 simulation supports tribute and campaign-troop obligation fulfillment, refusal, dispute, breach, and audit history through explicit serializable state and versioned commands/events.
Fulfillment draws from existing M2 grain/cash/labor/manpower scaffolds or explicit M3 placeholders without double-counting the same resource or obligation across a suzerain chain.
Obligation fulfillment reliability can vary with polity/vassal relationship context, administrative burden hooks, and character/office execution context while remaining deterministic and explainable.
Player and AI obligation actions use the same GameCommand validation and rejection reasons.
Scenario tests demonstrate at least one fulfilled tribute, one fulfilled troop obligation, one refusal/breach, and audit trails that identify source obligation and due period.
No full campaign warfare, full diplomacy, hidden global reputation system, client UI, formal historical content, or new production dependency is introduced.
required_tests: Property/invariant tests cover tribute/troop non-negative accounting, no duplicate fulfillment, stable due-period audit ordering, and refusal/breach reason consistency.
node -e JSON parse/shape check for M3-TRIBUTE-TROOP-OBLIGATIONS-001
notes: Troop fulfillment in M3 is obligation accounting and readiness scaffolding, not the M4 campaign/army movement loop.
Keep breach/audit history stable and readable for later UI reason display.
If implementation needs new diplomacy or war agreement semantics beyond vassal obligations, stop and route to systems_architect.
